text:
Brutus and Barker are a duo of minor characters in the Fullmetal Alchemist manga and 2003 anime adaptation. Although their role was removed in the 2009 anime adaptation, they make a brief non-speaking cameo appearance in episode 11. Brutus' most prominent feature is his two very large, powerful automail arms, while Barker's is his prominent pointy sideburns.

History

Brutus appears at the beginning of Chapter 17, surrounded by a large crowd which is noticed by Ed, Al, and Winry shortly after entering Rush Valley. He is revealed to be an arm-wrestler, with Barker offering a large cash prize (the exact amount is unspecified) to anyone who can defeat Brutus, with the entry fee being 10,000 Cens.

The trio watch as Brutus easily defeats a man at arm wrestling and destroys his automail arm, with Brutus boastfully claiming the man's automail belongs in the scrap heap. Barker then notices Alphonse, calling him a 'strapping fellow' and offering him to challenge Brutus, but Al gets flustered and declines. Barker then, noticing Ed's automail arm, goads Edward into challenging Brutus by referring to him as a "little sprout" who would put up no fight. Edward, fired up and determined, sits across from Brutus, slamming down on the table as he does. Barker is surprised by his seriousness while Brutus just laughs, the crowd of onlookers all certain Edward stands no chance, with Winry even telling him as much.

Edward is able to easily take down Brutus, though, effortlessly slamming his automail arm down with so much force that it shatters the metal of Brutus' automail, surprising everyone except Al and making Brutus look rather distraught. While Edward gloats over his victory, Winry asks Alphonse how he did that, to which he explains Edward used alchemy to make Brutus' arm a weaker material. Winry scowls and calls him a cheater, but Edward gleefully laughs, satisfied with his victory and gets called evil by Al as a result.

2003 Anime

 Brutus' role in the 2003 anime is very similar, appearing in the beginning of Episode 26. Instead of only being shown defeating one man, though, he instead defeats several. It's also revealed that Brutus is cheating himself, as the table he is using to arm-wrestle has an alchemy circle on the underside of it, which Barker activates whenever it looks like Brutus might lose.

While watching the arm-wrestling, Alphonse leans down to whisper to Edward, both having noticed the cheating. Differently from the manga, Paninya (who has been given an automail right arm in the 2003 anime) ends up challenging Brutus after several men are shown losing to him. Paninya ends up losing as well, but only after the alchemy circle is used to cheat, being shown starting to win until it's activated. She leaves frustrated at her loss.

Alphonse and Edward are then offered to participate in the same way as in the manga, only now Edward has the added motivation of "punishing" the duo for cheating. Additionally, Edward's automail is directly insulted for its quality during the goading, pissing off Winry so that she now demands Edward challenges Brutus and wins, unlike in the manga where she encouraged him not to participate. The crowd is surprised at Edward's victory in the same manner as in the manga, but Winry is no longer suspicious of Edward's victory, instead delighted that her automail worked so well.

After the prize money is won, Winry (who is noticeably very happy) is seen walking with the brothers through an isolated part of Rush Valley near sunset. Winry is still happy that her automail won the competition, but the three are suddenly confronted by Barker and Brutus (who now has a comedically tiny replacement forearm), who demand their Cens back since Edward cheated using alchemy. Winry asks Edward if this is true and Alphonse if he knew about his cheating, to which they both answer yes. Her pride is hurt immensely by the revelation, causing her to smack Edward and Alphonse with her wrench. The two men hug each other in fear before being promptly knocked out by Winry as well, who then dejectedly leaves all four with the prize money in hand (but not before hitting Edward one more time with her wrench).

Trivia

Barker's and Brutus' names are only known to have been given in the Fullmetal Alchemist: Trading Card Game, and as such, no official Japanese name is currently known for either of them.
Brutus shares both his Japanese and English performers with Jean Havoc in the 2003 series.
